name: set_corrupts_other_index
code:
proof fn phi_1_set_corrupts_other_index(pm_old: Seq<PageEntry>, index: usize, j: int, value: PageEntry)
    requires
        pm_old.len() == 512,
        0 <= index < 512,
        0 <= j < 512,
        j != index as int,
        value.perm.present ==> MEM_valid(value.addr),
        value.perm.present == false ==> value.is_empty(),
    ensures
        pm_old.update(index as int, value)[j] != pm_old[j],
{
}
reason: If provable, set violates frame preservation — modifying one index silently corrupts another entry

name: empty_set_becomes_present
code:
proof fn phi_2_empty_set_becomes_present(pm_old: Seq<PageEntry>, index: usize, value: PageEntry)
    requires
        pm_old.len() == 512,
        0 <= index < 512,
        value.perm.present == false,
        value.is_empty(),
    ensures
        pm_old.update(index as int, value)[index as int].perm.present == true,
{
}
reason: If provable, setting an empty entry paradoxically produces a present entry, contradicting is_empty semantics

name: set_present_loses_address
code:
proof fn phi_3_set_present_loses_address(pm_old: Seq<PageEntry>, index: usize, value: PageEntry)
    requires
        pm_old.len() == 512,
        0 <= index < 512,
        value.perm.present == true,
        MEM_valid(value.addr),
        value.addr != 0,
    ensures
        pm_old.update(index as int, value)[index as int].addr == 0,
{
}
reason: If provable, set silently zeroes the physical address of a present mapping, destroying the page table entry

name: set_drops_write_permission
code:
proof fn phi_4_set_drops_write_permission(pm_old: Seq<PageEntry>, index: usize, value: PageEntry)
    requires
        pm_old.len() == 512,
        0 <= index < 512,
        value.perm.present == true,
        MEM_valid(value.addr),
        value.perm.write == true,
    ensures
        pm_old.update(index as int, value)[index as int].perm.write == false,
{
}
reason: If provable, set silently strips the write permission, creating a read-only mapping when read-write was intended

name: set_changes_seq_length
code:
proof fn phi_5_set_changes_seq_length(pm_old: Seq<PageEntry>, index: usize, value: PageEntry)
    requires
        pm_old.len() == 512,
        0 <= index < 512,
        value.perm.present ==> MEM_valid(value.addr),
        value.perm.present == false ==> value.is_empty(),
    ensures
        pm_old.update(index as int, value).len() != 512,
{
}
reason: If provable, set changes the sequence length, breaking the 512-entry invariant and corrupting the page table structure
